name: Computer Science Engineering
description: A comprehensive program covering the theoretical foundations of computation, algorithmic problem-solving, software development, and modern computing systems.

subjects.name: Programming in C
subjects.description: Introduction to procedural programming using C language. Covers fundamentals of programming including variables, control structures, functions, arrays, pointers, and file handling.
learning_outcomes: Write structured C programs using appropriate control flow
Implement algorithms using arrays, pointers, and dynamic memory allocation
Develop file I/O applications
Debug and optimize C programs effectively
subjects.industry_relevance: C remains fundamental for embedded systems, operating systems, and performance-critical applications across industries.

subjects.name: Python Programming
subjects.description: Introduction to Python programming language with emphasis on readability, simplicity, and rapid prototyping. Covers data types, collections, functions, modules, and object-oriented features.
learning_outcomes: Write efficient Python code using built-in data structures
Apply object-oriented principles in Python
Use Python libraries for data manipulation and visualization
Build command-line applications and scripts
subjects.industry_relevance: Python is widely used in data science, web development, automation, and AI/ML across all tech sectors.

subjects.name: Java Programming
subjects.description: Comprehensive introduction to Java, a platform-independent object-oriented language. Covers syntax, OOP concepts, exception handling, multithreading, collections framework, and basic GUI programming.
learning_outcomes: Develop platform-independent Java applications
Implement multithreaded programs and handle concurrency
Utilize Java Collections Framework for data management
Build GUI applications using Swing or JavaFX
subjects.industry_relevance: Java powers enterprise applications, Android development, and large-scale backend systems in banking, e-commerce, and telecom.

subjects.name: Object Oriented Programming
subjects.description: Study of object-oriented programming paradigms including encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction. Implemented using C++ and Java with focus on design principles.
learning_outcomes: Apply OOP principles to design modular and reusable code
Implement inheritance hierarchies and polymorphic behavior
Use design patterns to solve common software design problems
Differentiate between procedural and object-oriented paradigms
subjects.industry_relevance: OOP is the backbone of modern software engineering used in virtually every large-scale application development project.

subjects.name: Data Structures
subjects.description: Systematic study of fundamental data structures including arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, hash tables, and heaps. Emphasizes analysis of operations and memory trade-offs.
learning_outcomes: Implement linear and non-linear data structures from scratch
Analyze time and space complexity of data structure operations
Select appropriate data structures for given problem constraints
Apply data structures in solving real-world computational problems
subjects.industry_relevance: Data structures are the foundation of technical interviews and essential for building efficient software systems at companies like Google, Microsoft, and Amazon.

subjects.name: Algorithms
subjects.description: Introduction to algorithm design and analysis covering sorting, searching, graph algorithms, and dynamic programming. Focuses on correctness proofs and asymptotic analysis.
learning_outcomes: Analyze algorithm efficiency using Big-O, Big-Theta, and Big-Omega notation
Design divide-and-conquer, greedy, and dynamic programming algorithms
Implement graph traversal and shortest path algorithms
Prove correctness of algorithms using loop invariants and induction
subjects.industry_relevance: Algorithmic thinking is critical for competitive programming and technical roles in product-based companies.

subjects.name: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
subjects.description: Advanced study of algorithm design techniques including amortized analysis, network flow, approximation algorithms, and NP-completeness. Covers advanced data structures and randomized algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Apply amortized analysis to evaluate algorithm performance
Design approximation and randomized algorithms for hard problems
Classify problems into complexity classes P, NP, NP-Complete
Implement network flow algorithms for optimization problems
subjects.industry_relevance: Advanced algorithm design is crucial for roles in quantitative finance, search engines, and large-scale data processing.

subjects.name: Database Management Systems
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of database systems covering relational model, SQL, normalization, transaction processing, concurrency control, and recovery mechanisms. Includes NoSQL database fundamentals.
learning_outcomes: Design normalized relational database schemas
Write complex SQL queries involving joins, subqueries, and aggregations
Implement transaction management and concurrency control protocols
Compare relational and NoSQL database systems for different use cases
subjects.industry_relevance: Database expertise is required for backend development, data engineering, and virtually all data-driven applications.

subjects.name: Operating Systems
subjects.description: Study of operating system principles including process management, memory management, file systems, I/O systems, and security. Includes hands-on experience with Linux kernel concepts.
learning_outcomes: Implement CPU scheduling and page replacement algorithms
Design and implement a simple file system
Analyze process synchronization problems and solutions
Understand memory management techniques including paging and segmentation
subjects.industry_relevance: OS knowledge is essential for systems programming, cloud infrastructure, and performance engineering roles.

subjects.name: Computer Networks
subjects.description: Study of computer network architectures, protocols, and applications following the OSI and TCP/IP models. Covers physical layer through application layer with focus on internet protocols.
learning_outcomes: Explain the OSI and TCP/IP protocol stacks and their layers
Design and configure IP addressing and subnetting schemes
Analyze network performance using throughput and latency metrics
Implement client-server applications using socket programming
subjects.industry_relevance: Networking is foundational for cloud computing, IoT, cybersecurity, and telecommunications industries.

subjects.name: Software Engineering
subjects.description: Systematic approach to software development covering requirements engineering, design methodologies, testing, maintenance, and project management. Includes Agile and DevOps practices.
learning_outcomes: Apply Agile and Scrum methodologies in software projects
Design software architectures using UML and design patterns
Implement comprehensive testing strategies including unit, integration, and system tests
Manage software projects using version control and CI/CD pipelines
subjects.industry_relevance: Software engineering principles are universally applied across all tech companies building reliable and scalable products.

subjects.name: Compiler Design
subjects.description: Principles and techniques for designing and implementing compilers. Covers lexical analysis, parsing, syntax-directed translation, intermediate code generation, optimization, and code generation.
learning_outcomes: Design lexical analyzers using regular expressions and finite automata
Implement top-down and bottom-up parsing techniques
Generate intermediate code and apply optimization techniques
Build a working compiler for a subset of a programming language
subjects.industry_relevance: Compiler expertise is valuable in programming language design, high-performance computing, and tooling at companies like Intel, LLVM, and JetBrains.

subjects.name: Computer Organization
subjects.description: Study of the structure and behavior of computer systems. Covers digital logic, instruction set architecture, CPU design, memory hierarchy, pipelining, and I/O organization.
learning_outcomes: Design combinational and sequential digital circuits
Explain instruction execution pipeline and hazards
Analyze memory hierarchy performance including cache and virtual memory
Evaluate different CPU architectures including RISC and CISC
subjects.industry_relevance: Computer organization knowledge is critical for hardware design, embedded systems, and performance optimization roles.

subjects.name: Digital Logic
subjects.description: Fundamentals of digital systems including number systems, Boolean algebra, combinational and sequential logic circuits, and basic digital components like multiplexers, decoders, and flip-flops.
learning_outcomes: Simplify Boolean expressions using Karnaugh maps and algebraic manipulation
Design combinational circuits for arithmetic and logic operations
Build sequential circuits including registers and counters
Analyze and design synchronous sequential circuits
subjects.industry_relevance: Digital logic is foundational for VLSI design, FPGA programming, and embedded systems engineering.

subjects.name: Theory of Computation
subjects.description: Mathematical study of computation covering automata theory, formal languages, Turing machines, decidability, and computational complexity. Explores the limits of what computers can solve.
learning_outcomes: Design finite automata, pushdown automata, and Turing machines
Prove undecidability using reduction techniques
Classify languages within Chomsky hierarchy
Analyze problems in terms of computability and complexity
subjects.industry_relevance: Theoretical foundations are essential for advanced roles in cryptography, programming languages, and formal verification.

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence
subjects.description: Introduction to AI principles including search algorithms, knowledge representation, reasoning under uncertainty, and intelligent agents. Includes foundational concepts in machine learning.
learning_outcomes: Implement informed and uninformed search algorithms
Represent knowledge using logic and probabilistic models
Design intelligent agents for game playing and problem solving
Apply Bayesian networks for reasoning under uncertainty
subjects.industry_relevance: AI is transforming industries from healthcare to finance with autonomous systems, recommendation engines, and intelligent automation.

subjects.name: Machine Learning
subjects.description: Study of algorithms that enable computers to learn from data. Covers supervised learning, unsupervised learning, ensemble methods, and model evaluation techniques with practical implementations.
learning_outcomes: Implement linear and logistic regression for prediction tasks
Build decision trees, random forests, and SVM classifiers
Apply clustering algorithms including k-means and DBSCAN
Evaluate model performance using cross-validation and appropriate metrics
subjects.industry_relevance: Machine learning skills are highly sought after in tech, finance, healthcare, and e-commerce for building predictive systems.

subjects.name: Cloud Computing
subjects.description: Study of cloud computing models including IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS. Covers virtualization, distributed storage, cloud security, and major cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, and GCP.
learning_outcomes: Deploy applications on AWS EC2, S3, and Lambda services
Design cloud-native architectures using microservices
Implement auto-scaling and load balancing strategies
Apply cloud security best practices and IAM policies
subjects.industry_relevance: Cloud computing is a cornerstone of modern IT infrastructure with organizations migrating to cloud platforms globally.

subjects.name: Cyber Security
subjects.description: Study of security principles, threats, and countermeasures in computing systems. Covers cryptography, network security, web security, ethical hacking, and security policies.
learning_outcomes: Identify common vulnerabilities including SQL injection and XSS
Implement cryptographic protocols for secure communication
Conduct penetration testing using industry-standard tools
Design security policies and incident response strategies
subjects.industry_relevance: Cybersecurity is critical across all industries with rising threats and regulatory compliance requirements.

subjects.name: Web Technologies
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of web development covering HTML, CSS, JavaScript, frontend frameworks, server-side programming, REST APIs, and modern web application architecture.
learning_outcomes: Build responsive web interfaces using HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript
Develop single-page applications using React or Angular
Create RESTful APIs with Node.js and Express
Implement authentication and session management in web apps
subjects.industry_relevance: Web development is the most in-demand skill across startups and enterprises building digital products and services.

subjects.name: Mobile App Development
subjects.description: Study of mobile application development for Android and iOS platforms. Covers native development with Kotlin/Swift and cross-platform frameworks like Flutter and React Native.
learning_outcomes: Build native Android applications using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose
Develop iOS applications using Swift and SwiftUI
Create cross-platform apps using Flutter or React Native
Integrate mobile apps with backend APIs and cloud services
subjects.industry_relevance: Mobile app development is a multi-billion dollar industry essential for consumer engagement and enterprise mobility.

subjects.name: Distributed Systems
subjects.description: Study of distributed computing principles including remote procedure calls, distributed file systems, consensus algorithms, replication, and fault tolerance in large-scale systems.
learning_outcomes: Implement distributed consensus using Paxos or Raft algorithms
Design distributed hash tables and peer-to-peer systems
Apply CAP theorem to design trade-offs in distributed databases
Build fault-tolerant distributed applications using message passing
subjects.industry_relevance: Distributed systems expertise is crucial for building scalable services at companies like Google, Amazon, and Netflix.

subjects.name: Big Data
subjects.description: Technologies and techniques for processing and analyzing massive datasets. Covers Hadoop ecosystem, MapReduce, Spark, distributed file systems, and real-time stream processing.
learning_outcomes: Process large datasets using Hadoop MapReduce and Apache Spark
Design data pipelines for batch and stream processing
Implement distributed data storage with HDFS and NoSQL databases
Analyze streaming data using Apache Kafka and Flink
subjects.industry_relevance: Big data technologies power data-driven decision making in e-commerce, finance, healthcare, and social media platforms.

subjects.name: Data Mining
subjects.description: Study of techniques for discovering patterns in large datasets. Covers association rules, classification, clustering, anomaly detection, and web mining with practical applications.
learning_outcomes: Discover frequent patterns and association rules using Apriori and FP-Growth
Apply classification techniques including decision trees and neural networks
Detect anomalies in large datasets using statistical and distance-based methods
Mine text and web data for information extraction
subjects.industry_relevance: Data mining drives business intelligence, recommendation systems, and customer analytics across industries.

subjects.name: DevOps
subjects.description: Study of DevOps culture, practices, and tools for continuous integration, continuous delivery, infrastructure as code, monitoring, and automation in software development lifecycle.
learning_outcomes: Set up CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or GitLab CI
Containerize applications using Docker and orchestrate with Kubernetes
Manage infrastructure using Terraform and configuration management tools
Implement monitoring and logging using Prometheus and ELK stack
subjects.industry_relevance: DevOps practices are adopted by virtually all tech companies to accelerate software delivery and improve reliability.

subjects.name: Blockchain
subjects.description: Study of blockchain technology including distributed ledger, consensus mechanisms, smart contracts, and decentralized applications. Covers Ethereum, Hyperledger, and cryptocurrency fundamentals.
learning_outcomes: Explain blockchain architecture and consensus algorithms
Develop and deploy smart contracts using Solidity
Build decentralized applications on Ethereum platform
Analyze security considerations and scalability challenges in blockchain
subjects.industry_relevance: Blockchain technology is transforming finance, supply chain, healthcare, and digital identity management.

subjects.name: Internet of Things
subjects.description: Study of interconnected smart devices, sensors, and actuators. Covers IoT architectures, communication protocols, embedded systems, cloud integration, and IoT security challenges.
learning_outcomes: Design IoT system architectures with sensors and actuators
Implement communication using MQTT, CoAP, and BLE protocols
Develop firmware for microcontrollers like Arduino and ESP32
Integrate IoT devices with cloud platforms for data analytics
subjects.industry_relevance: IoT is driving innovation in smart homes, industrial automation, healthcare monitoring, and smart cities.
